facts: The patient's name is Jack.
The patient has neck pain.
The neck pain started after a car accident.
The car accident occurred about a week ago.
The patient's neck went forward quickly during the car accident.
The patient's head went forward quickly during the car accident.
The patient hit their back during the car accident.
The patient experiences pain in the back of their neck.
The neck pain is not constant.
The neck pain occurs when the patient moves their head.
The patient rates their neck pain as a 7 out of 10.
The patient has tried ibuprofen for pain relief.
Ibuprofen helped the patient's pain a little bit.
The patient rates the effectiveness of ibuprofen as 5 out of 10.
The patient's neck pain has stayed the same since the car accident.
The patient's neck pain gets worse when they move their neck.
The patient can mostly turn their neck from side to side.
The patient experiences pain when turning their neck.
The patient has had headaches.
The patient had headaches early on after the car accident.
The patient has not had headaches lately.
The patient does not have hearing problems.
The patient does not have visual disturbances.
The patient has played sports before.
The patient played football.
The patient played football years ago.
The patient did not have neck issues while playing football.
The patient did not go to the emergency room after the car accident.
The patient did not want to pay for an emergency room visit.
The patient does not know if they had swelling or bruising on their neck.
The patient cannot see the back of their neck.
The doctor performed a physical exam on the patient.
The patient experiences pain when the doctor presses on the side of their neck.
The patient does not experience pain when the doctor presses on their back or shoulders.
The patient experiences pain when flexing their neck.
The patient experiences pain when extending their neck.
The patient experiences severe pain when extending their neck.
The patient experiences pain when rotating their neck.
The patient cannot touch their ear to their shoulder.
The patient had an x-ray.
The x-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
The doctor diagnosed the patient with neck strain.
The doctor prescribed Motrin.
Motrin is an anti-inflammatory.
The Motrin dose is 600 milligrams.
Motrin is taken every 6 to 8 hours.
The doctor prescribed Flexeril.
Flexeril is a muscle relaxant.
The Flexeril dose is 10 milligrams.
Flexeril is taken every 12 hours.
Flexeril is taken as needed.
The doctor ordered an MRI for the patient.
The doctor wants to check for a concussion.
The patient's condition is commonly referred to as whiplash.
The doctor wants the patient to relax their neck.
The doctor wants the patient to avoid straining their neck.
The doctor may refer the patient to physical rehab.
The doctor may refer the patient to a chiropractor.
The doctor wants to check for nerve impingement.
The patient has heard that chiropractors can be effective.
The doctor has recommended chiropractors with good reputations.
The patient is open to alternative options.
The doctor offered physical therapy as an alternative option.
The patient does not have any questions.
